scheme_name: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Storage of Minimum 50-Ton Capacity - Haryana
page_title: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Storage of Minimum 50-Ton Capacity - Haryana

details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Ice Plant/Cold Storage of Minimum 50-Ton Capacity , introduced by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to provide financial assistance for setting up ice plants or cold storage units with a minimum capacity of 50 tons, supporting cold chain infrastructure under PMMSY. The aim is to reduce post-harvest losses and improve fish quality. This is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme, funded in the ratio of: 50% by the Central Government, and 50% by the State Government. The beneficiary must operate and maintain the plant and ensure supply of ice to fishers at affordable prices.
benefits: A 40% subsidy is available for the General Category.
A 60% subsidy is offered for SC (Scheduled Caste) and Women beneficiaries.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
The applicant must own land or hold a registered lease for a minimum period of 10 years.
The applicant must submit an undertaking within the DPR/SCP, accepting full responsibility for operating and maintaining the unit.
The applicant must provide ice at affordable prices to fishers.
The applicant must submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR) or Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) in accordance with PMMSY guidelines.
The applicant must hold a valid training certificate in ice plant or cold storage operation and maintenance.
The applicant must ensure the land is free from legal issues and must provide a valid land title.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type SARAL and send to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application/ticket.

documents_required: Date of Birth Proof (Voter ID, Aadhaar, Matriculation Certificate)
Caste Certificate (SC/Women applicants – issued by Tehsildar/SDM)
Training Certificate in cold storage/ice plant operations
Land Records (Jamabandi, Fard, Registered Patta with clear title)
Photographs of cold storage with beneficiary
Bank Account Details and PAN Card
CA Certificate for GST/IT compliance of bills
Bills > ₹50,000 must follow GST/IT/e-way bill norms

faqs.question: As a General Category applicant, how much subsidy can I receive for this project?
faqs.answer: You are eligible for a subsidy of 40% of the total project cost under this scheme.

faqs.question: If I belong to the SC category or I’m a woman, what subsidy am I eligible for?
faqs.answer: You are eligible for a subsidy of 60% of the project cost if you are from the SC category or are a woman applicant.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory to have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, having a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) is a mandatory requirement for application.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I am setting up the project on leased land?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can apply if the land is on a registered lease for a minimum period of 10 years.

faqs.question: What documents can be submitted as proof of date of birth?
faqs.answer: You can submit a Voter Card, Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, or Matriculation Certificate as proof of date of birth.

faqs.question: Do I need to provide any photographs while applying?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must submit photographs of the cold storage along with the beneficiary.

faqs.question: How long will it take for my application to be processed?
faqs.answer: The application will be processed within 50 days as per the Right to Service (RTS) time limit.

faqs.question: Is any local approval or NOC required for the unit?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must obtain NOCs from the Gram Sabha or Municipal Corporation/Authority where your unit is located.

faqs.question: Do I need to attach a site plan while submitting the application?
faqs.answer: Yes, a clear Site Plan or Area Site Plan must be attached along with your subsidy case.
